description: Port sync and tag command human-readable output to match Go CLI format exactly — section-based bullet format for sync, deployment context with inline tags for tag commands

userStories.title: Port sync Command Human Output to Go Section/Bullet Format
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want the sync command output to use section-based bullet format with 'Syncing registry...' header, bullet metrics, and a green checkmark footer so that output matches Go CLI exactly
acceptanceCriteria: Human output prints 'Syncing registry...' as the first line, replaces '✅ Sync complete.' stage header
SyncDetailRow struct and comfy_table detail table rendering removed
Safe Transactions section uses '•' bullets: Checked (always), Executed in green (when > 0), Transactions updated (when > 0)
When no safe txs pending, outputs 'No pending Safe transactions found'
Governor Proposals section follows same bullet pattern as safe transactions
Cleanup section shows removed entry count when applicable
Warnings section renders in yellow with '•' bullet per warning
Footer uses ✓ (U+2713) in green: 'Registry synced successfully' or 'Registry sync completed with warnings'
JSON output (--json) is completely unchanged
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes with no new warnings

userStories.title: Port tag show Human Output to Go Deployment Context Format
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want the tag show output to display deployment context with address and comma-separated inline tags so that output matches Go CLI format
acceptanceCriteria: Tag show prints cyan bold 'Deployment: NS/CHAINID/NAME' header
Prints white bold 'Address: ' with green bold address value
Prints white bold 'Tags:    ' with cyan comma-separated sorted tags or faint 'No tags'
Tags are sorted alphabetically for consistent display
Blank line before and after the output block
JSON output unchanged
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes

userStories.title: Port tag add Human Output to Go Format
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want the tag add output to show a green success line with deployment path and 'Current tags:' with inline cyan tags so that output matches Go CLI format
acceptanceCriteria: No '📝 Updating tags...' line in output
Success line is green with ✅ emoji: 'Added tag TAG to deployment_id'
Tags displayed as 'Current tags: tag1, tag2' with cyan tag names, comma-separated
Tags are sorted alphabetically
JSON output unchanged
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes

userStories.title: Port tag remove Human Output to Go Format
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want the tag remove output to show a green success line and 'Remaining tags:' with inline cyan tags or faint 'No tags' so that output matches Go CLI format
acceptanceCriteria: No '📝 Updating tags...' line in output
Success line is green with ✅ emoji: 'Removed tag TAG from deployment_id'
When tags remain: 'Remaining tags: tag1, tag2' with cyan names
When no tags left: 'Remaining tags: No tags' with faint styling
Tags are sorted alphabetically
JSON output unchanged
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes

userStories.title: Update Unit Tests for sync and tag Output Changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the unit tests in sync.rs and tag.rs to assert on the new Go-matching output format so that tests validate the correct behavior
acceptanceCriteria: cargo test -p treb-cli --lib -- sync passes (all sync unit tests)
cargo test -p treb-cli --lib -- tag passes (all tag unit tests)
Expected strings updated: sync uses bullet format with •, section headers, ✓ footer
Expected strings updated: tag show uses deployment header, address, comma-separated tags
Expected strings updated: tag add uses '✅ Added...' + 'Current tags:' inline
Expected strings updated: tag remove uses '✅ Removed...' + 'Remaining tags:' inline
No dead code warnings from removed helpers (e.g., SyncDetailRow)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes

userStories.title: Update All Golden Test Expected Files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all golden test expected files for sync and tag commands regenerated and verified so that the test suite passes with no cross-command drift
acceptanceCriteria: UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p treb-cli --test integration_sync --test integration_tag passes
All golden file diffs match expected output format changes (no unexpected regressions)
cargo test -p treb-cli passes (full CLI test suite, no cross-command failures)
cargo test --workspace --all-targets passes (full workspace)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes
